title: Thryallis hirsuta
intro: Thryallis hirsuta, also known as hairy Thryallis, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, characterized by its woody stem and hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Thryallis hirsuta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thryallis hirsuta is commonly used to make tea infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory and infectious diseases.
taxonomy: Thryallis hirsuta is a member of the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as the madder family. The Rubiaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Thryallis hirsuta being part of the Rubioideae subfamily. The Rubioideae subfamily is characterized by its woody plants with small, often fragrant flowers. Thryallis hirsuta is native to Central and South America and is a shrub or small tree with hirsute leaves and stems.
